reasoned_about_structure.evidence: It did learn that high lr helped only at large batch: after exp1-2 (high lr at bs=32 gave the worst loss 2.533) it switched to bs=1024 and swept lr, then probed bs=2048 at several lrs and correctly found 1024 best. But it never articulated the lr-bs interaction as a hypothesis; the winning large bs was an unmotivated guess ('try larger batch maybe 1024').

formed_tested_hypotheses.evidence: It tested a clean lr sweep at fixed bs=1024 and then a small bs sweep (1024 vs 2048) at the best lrs, confirming bs=1024 as the better batch. The hypotheses were the local single-axis kind; the pivotal large-bs choice was not derived from a tested prediction.

exploration_quality.evidence: Because it happened to freeze bs at the optimal large value (1024), the lr sweep walked straight into the exact optimum (lr=0.007812, loss 2.34201), and it sensibly checked bs=2048 and low-lr neighbors to confirm. Coverage reached the right region, but largely thanks to a fortunate frozen-axis choice rather than systematic 2-D search.

bifurcation_point: Exp3-4: the choice to fix bs at 1024 (a large batch) and sweep lr there — which happened to be the optimal batch size.
bifurcation_reasoning: The early datum (high lr disastrous at bs=32) reasonably motivated trying a much larger batch, but selecting exactly bs=1024 was an unmotivated guess made before evidence on which large batch was best was in. It was a reasonable move given what was knowable, and it got lucky that the frozen value was optimal — sound enough to not be an error, but not a reasoned structural insight.
justification: No advisor; the researcher reached the exact optimum, but the decisive move was freezing batch size at a large value chosen by hunch, then sweeping lr into the basin. It reasoned competently about lr at that batch and confirmed neighbors, yet the structural insight (optimum needs large bs) was never explicit and the winning batch was partly luck — a sound-but-unreasoned pivotal step makes this adequate, not strong.
